summary: Production-grade growth system with 127 tracked analytics events, 7 lifecycle stages, 11 scheduled commands, 13+ notification types, multi-tier billing with Redis-locked mutations, and comprehensive admin analytics. Key gaps: 11 untested lifecycle commands, missing LeadScoringService/LifecycleService tests, win-back dedup inefficiency, and no automated A/B variant analysis.

lifecycle_email_sequences.summary: 11 lifecycle commands covering welcome, onboarding, trial nudges, dunning, re-engagement, win-back, and lead qualification. EmailSendLog provides atomic deduplication with unique constraint. All notifications implement ShouldQueue and HasUnsubscribeLink.
strengths: EmailSendLog unique constraint prevents duplicate sends atomically
Cross-sequence dedup between welcome and onboarding (email #2 overlap prevention)
Marketing opt-out checked in 6/7 campaign commands via UserSetting
All lifecycle notifications queued async with retry backoff
HasUnsubscribeLink trait on all marketing notifications with signed URLs
Deprecated SendTrialEndingReminders removed from schedule to prevent double-sends
Lifetime idempotency on CheckExpiredTrials via AuditLog existence check

findings.title: SendWinBackEmails uses inefficient notification table dedup
findings.description: SendWinBackEmails.php lines 107-115 queries ALL notifications and filters in PHP instead of using EmailSendLog pattern. Loads potentially thousands of records per user. No unique constraint prevents race-condition duplicates.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to EmailSendLog dedup pattern matching other lifecycle commands

findings.title: IncompletePaymentReminder uses fragile time-window dedup
findings.description: CheckIncompletePayments.php lines 46-49 uses 30-minute notification existence window for dedup. If scheduler restarts within window, duplicates possible.
findings.recommendation: Consider EmailSendLog or notification table unique constraint

findings.title: UpgradeNudgeNotification bypasses marketing opt-out
findings.description: LeadQualifiedListener dispatches UpgradeNudgeNotification without checking marketing_emails opt-out. Uses 30-day cache suppression but not user preference.
findings.recommendation: Add marketing opt-out check before sending UpgradeNudgeNotification

findings.title: Email sequence schedules hardcoded in each command
findings.description: Each lifecycle command defines its own EMAIL_SCHEDULE constant. No centralized config for A/B testing different timings.
findings.recommendation: Extract to config/email-sequences.php for centralized management

findings.title: No queue rate limiting configured for notification dispatch
findings.description: All notifications use Queueable but no queue rate limiting in config/queue.php. Could send thousands of notifications in parallel.
findings.recommendation: Configure queue throttling for notification channels

analytics_tracking.summary: 127 tracked events across 12 categories with GA4 Measurement Protocol integration. Type-safe frontend tracking with React hooks. PII blocklist (35+ keys), GDPR consent gating, IP anonymization, and cross-platform event sync validated by automated tests.
strengths: 127 events defined in AnalyticsEvent enum covering full user lifecycle
53 shared events synced between PHP and TypeScript with automated parity tests
GA4 Measurement Protocol with 35+ PII key blocklist (defense-in-depth)
GDPR-compliant consent gating with legitimate interest for server-side events
IP anonymization for non-security events (last octet zeroed)
Type-safe frontend event properties via TypeScript mapped types with exhaustiveness check
Pre-consent event queue (max 20) with 5-second retry flush on gtag availability
First-touch UTM attribution captured at middleware level
PQL threshold signals at 50%/80%/100% usage with daily per-user dedup
3 pre-configured GA4 funnels: signup (7d), trial-to-paid (30d), login (1d)
Async job dispatch with retry (2 attempts, 10s/60s backoff)
AuditService enriches events with plan_tier, signup_cohort, is_activated context

findings.title: UTM capture is first-touch only
findings.description: CaptureUtmParameters middleware stores UTM data only if session doesn't already have utm_data. Last-touch attribution not available for multi-visit conversion analysis.
findings.recommendation: Consider storing both first-touch and last-touch UTM for richer attribution

findings.title: GA4 forwards only 10 high-value events
findings.description: AuditService.GA4_FORWARDED_EVENTS limits server-side GA4 to 10 lifecycle events. Remaining 117 events are audit-log only.
findings.recommendation: Intentional design for cost control. Review periodically as analytics needs evolve.

billing_monetization.summary: 5-tier billing system (free/pro/pro_team/team/enterprise) with Redis-locked subscription mutations, A/B price testing, swap proration preview, retention coupon, 7-day grace period enforcement, and comprehensive Stripe webhook handling with out-of-order rejection.
strengths: Redis-locked subscription mutations (35s timeout) prevent concurrent race conditions
Out-of-order webhook rejection via last_webhook_at timestamp comparison
Atomic A/B variant pricing: display price and Stripe price ID always swapped together
Swap proration preview via Stripe invoice preview API before commitment
7-day grace period with automatic downgrade (EnforceGracePeriod command)
Reliable dunning timing via past_due_since timestamp (not event window)
Invoice URL sanitization: HTTPS + stripe.com domain validation only
Coupon validation with 60-second cache to reduce Stripe API calls
Friendly Stripe error messages for card_declined, expired_card, etc.
Pro Team tier bridges $19 Pro to $147 Team minimum (reduces price cliff)
Incomplete payment reminders at 1h/12h/urgent escalation
Voluntary vs involuntary churn tracking in webhook handler
Retention coupon endpoint with strict rate limiting (3/60min)

findings.title: Metered billing scaffolded but removed
findings.description: meter_id and meter_event_name columns were added then dropped. No MeterEventService exists. Usage-based billing not implemented.
findings.recommendation: Intentional removal. Re-implement when product requires usage-based pricing.

findings.title: Retention coupon not automatically offered on cancel flow
findings.description: Retention coupon applied via manual API endpoint (SubscriptionController lines 445-495). No automatic trigger during cancellation flow.
findings.recommendation: Consider auto-offering retention coupon in cancel confirmation dialog

findings.title: No automated A/B variant conversion analysis
findings.description: Pro tier supports stripe_price_monthly_variant for A/B testing. Variant assignment logged but no automated conversion comparison command exists.
findings.recommendation: Build variant analysis query or dashboard widget

findings.title: Enterprise tier requires custom pricing
findings.description: Enterprise has no default display prices. Requires manual Stripe price ID configuration. Self-serve checkout possible only if Stripe prices configured.
findings.recommendation: Intentional design for custom enterprise deals.

onboarding_activation.summary: 3-step onboarding wizard (profile, preferences, feature discovery) with use-case segmentation, lifecycle stage transition to ACTIVATED, middleware enforcement, and 3-email reminder sequence. Engagement scoring awards 25/100 points for completion.
strengths: 3-step wizard with use-case segmentation (SaaS/Agency/Internal/Evaluating)
Context-aware action cards based on use_case and enabled features
EnsureOnboardingCompleted middleware blocks dashboard until completion
Lifecycle stage transition to ACTIVATED with audit trail
Analytics events: ONBOARDING_STARTED, ONBOARDING_STEP_COMPLETED, ONBOARDING_COMPLETED
25-point engagement score bonus for completion
Cross-sequence dedup between welcome and onboarding emails
Email verification warning displayed in onboarding step 3
Smart email #3 suppression if user has recent activity (last 3 days)

findings.title: No guided tour or tooltip system
findings.description: Project relies on onboarding wizard and email sequences. No in-app guided tour or contextual tooltip system for feature discovery.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding product tour for complex features (webhooks, API tokens)

findings.title: No activation milestone tracking beyond onboarding completion
findings.description: ACTIVATION_MILESTONE event defined in AnalyticsEvent enum but no visible triggers. Activation is binary (completed/not) rather than progressive.
findings.recommendation: Implement progressive activation milestones (first API call, first webhook, first export)

findings.title: Onboarding completion depends on user_settings feature
findings.description: EnsureOnboardingCompleted middleware skips enforcement if user_settings feature is disabled. Onboarding state cannot be persisted without it.
findings.recommendation: Document this dependency in feature flag docs

scoring_segmentation.summary: Three scoring services (engagement 0-100, health 0-100, lead composite) with batch optimization. 7 lifecycle stages with transition audit trail. MQL/SQL qualification events at 60/80 thresholds. Health distribution brackets for at-risk detection.
strengths: Batch-optimized scoring: EngagementScoringService uses 4 queries for N users
CustomerHealthService pre-warms caches for login counts and billing status
Composite lead scoring: 50% engagement + 30% health + 20% PQL signal
PQL signal from LIMIT_THRESHOLD_80 events (product usage = buying intent)
Lifecycle stage transitions audited with days_in_previous_stage metadata
Stage velocity analytics: median days between stage pairs (cached)
Health distribution brackets: critical/at_risk/moderate/healthy
D7 and D30 retention rates calculated from verified user cohorts
Automatic AT_RISK transition on invoice.payment_failed webhook
Automatic win-back notification queued on CHURNED transition (24h delay)

findings.title: EXPANSION stage defined but no transition logic exists
findings.description: LifecycleStage::EXPANSION is defined in enum and funnel order but no code triggers transition to EXPANSION. Users who upgrade plans stay in PAYING.
findings.recommendation: Implement EXPANSION transition on plan upgrade (swap to higher tier)

findings.title: No AT_RISK recovery transition back to PAYING
findings.description: Users transition to AT_RISK on payment failure but no explicit transition back to PAYING on payment recovery (invoice.payment_succeeded).
findings.recommendation: Add PAYING transition in handleInvoicePaymentSucceeded when past_due_since is cleared

findings.title: Health score billing component awards 0 points to free users
findings.description: Free users get 0/25 billing points, capping their max health score at 75. This may unfairly classify free users as at-risk.
findings.recommendation: Consider separate health brackets for free vs paid users

findings.title: No cohort-based segmentation beyond signup week
findings.description: getCohortRetention groups by signup week only. No segmentation by acquisition channel, use_case, or plan tier.
findings.recommendation: Add cohort dimensions for richer retention analysis

lead.pql_signal: 20 points if LIMIT_THRESHOLD_80 in last 30 days

admin_notifications.summary: Comprehensive admin panel with growth metrics dashboard, user lifecycle management, billing analytics (MRR/churn/trial conversion/cohort retention), NPS scoring, product analytics, data health checks, and automated health alerts every 15 minutes.
strengths: Admin dashboard shows lifecycle funnel, D7/D30 retention, 30-day signup chart
Billing dashboard: MRR, churn rate, trial conversion, cohort retention matrix
NPS scoring with promoter/passive/detractor categorization and CSV export
Product analytics: signup trend, onboarding funnel, activation rate, feature adoption
User detail page shows lifecycle stage history, billing status, engagement score
Health alerts every 15 minutes: failed jobs, webhook failure rate, retention thresholds
Data health checks: orphaned tokens, audit logs, stale webhooks, orphaned subscriptions
Email send log admin view with filtering and export
Admin announcement broadcasting via BroadcastAnnouncementJob
Audit log system with IP anonymization for non-security events
Read notification pruning (30-day retention, daily schedule)

findings.title: Health alerts check retention but not MRR drop or churn spike
findings.description: config/analytics-thresholds.php defines churn_rate and mrr_drop_percent thresholds but AdminHealthAlertCommand only checks failed_jobs, health_status, webhook_failure_rate, d7_retention, d30_retention. MRR/churn thresholds are defined but not consumed.
findings.recommendation: Add MRR drop and churn rate checks to health alert command

findings.title: Notification system disabled by default
findings.description: FEATURE_NOTIFICATIONS defaults to false. In-app notification center requires explicit enablement.
findings.recommendation: Document that lifecycle emails work without this flag (they use mail channel directly)

admin_dashboards: Main dashboard (users, retention, signups, lifecycle funnel)
Billing dashboard (MRR, churn, trial conversion, cohort retention)
Product analytics (signup trend, onboarding funnel, feature adoption)
Email send logs (sequence filtering, user search, export)
Audit logs (event filtering, date range, IP search, export)

test_coverage.summary: ~450 growth-related tests across billing (198), notifications (69), lifecycle commands (34), admin (39+), onboarding (26), and services (31). Critical gaps in lifecycle command coverage (6/17 tested) and core services (3/18 tested).
strengths: 198 billing tests covering webhooks, subscription states, pricing, concurrency, coupons
69 notification tests including unsubscribe links, dunning escalation, onboarding reminders
18 AnalyticsGateway unit tests covering GA4 dispatch, PII sanitization, batch logic
Frontend/backend event sync validated by automated parity tests (AnalyticsEventSyncTest)
5 contract tests for feature flag resolution precedence
Billing tests cover incomplete payments, seat management, tier resolution, cache invalidation

findings.title: 11 lifecycle commands have no tests
findings.description: Only 6/17 lifecycle commands have test coverage. Missing: CheckExpiredTrials, QualifyLeads, ComputeUserScores, SendOnboardingReminders, CheckIncompletePayments, SendDunningReminders, EnforceGracePeriod, PruneAuditLogs, PruneOldWebhookDeliveries, PruneReadNotifications, PruneStaleWebhookDeliveries.
findings.recommendation: Prioritize tests for CheckExpiredTrials, QualifyLeads, ComputeUserScores, SendDunningReminders, EnforceGracePeriod

findings.title: LeadScoringService has no tests
findings.description: Core lead qualification formula (engagement*0.5 + health*0.3 + pql*0.2) and MQL/SQL thresholds are untested.
findings.recommendation: Create LeadScoringServiceTest with composite formula validation and threshold crossing tests

findings.title: LifecycleService has no tests
findings.description: Stage transitions, UserStageHistory recording, days_in_previous_stage calculation, and CHURNED win-back side-effect are untested.
findings.recommendation: Create LifecycleServiceTest covering transitions, history recording, and churn side-effects

findings.title: Only 3/18 scoring/health services have tests
findings.description: AnalyticsGatewayTest (18), EngagementScoringServiceTest (4), CustomerHealthServiceTest (9) exist. ProductAnalyticsService, DataHealthService, AdminBillingStatsService, PlanLimitService, and others untested.
findings.recommendation: Add tests for PlanLimitService (trial start, limit checking, PQL signals) and AdminBillingStatsService (MRR calculation)

priority_test_gaps: QualifyLeads — lead score computation + MQL/SQL events

configuration_feature_flags.summary: 11 feature flags in config/features.php with user-specific overrides via FeatureFlagService. All billing commands gated by feature flags. phpunit.xml enables all features for testing. Comprehensive .env.example with 60+ growth-related variables.
strengths: Centralized feature flag config with env var overrides
User-specific overrides via FeatureFlagService::resolve()
5 contract tests validate flag resolution precedence
All billing scheduled commands gated by features.billing.enabled
Webhook out-of-order rejection via last_webhook_at sequence tracking
phpunit.xml enables all features for comprehensive test coverage
Pro tier A/B testing with atomic variant price swapping
Social auth auto-detection by CLIENT_ID presence
Plan limits configurable per tier via env vars
billing.coming_soon flag for soft-launching pricing page

findings.title: Trial command gated by plans.trial.enabled, not features.billing.enabled
findings.description: trials:check-expired uses config('plans.trial.enabled') while other billing commands use config('features.billing.enabled'). Different gate hierarchies.
findings.recommendation: Document that trial system is independent of billing feature flag

findings.title: analytics-thresholds.php MRR/churn thresholds not consumed by health alerts
findings.description: Thresholds for churn_rate and mrr_drop_percent are defined but AdminHealthAlertCommand doesn't check them.
findings.recommendation: Wire MRR/churn threshold checks into health alert command

findings.title: No centralized feature flag dependency documentation
findings.description: Onboarding depends on user_settings. Social auth auto-detects from env vars. These implicit dependencies are not centrally documented.
findings.recommendation: Add dependency graph to docs/FEATURE_FLAGS.md

cross_cutting_findings.title: Core growth services lack test coverage
cross_cutting_findings.description: LeadScoringService (composite formula), LifecycleService (stage transitions), and 11/17 lifecycle commands have no tests. These are critical growth pipeline components.
cross_cutting_findings.impact: Regressions in scoring formulas or lifecycle transitions would be undetected
cross_cutting_findings.recommendation: Create test files for LeadScoringService, LifecycleService, CheckExpiredTrials, QualifyLeads, ComputeUserScores, SendDunningReminders, EnforceGracePeriod

cross_cutting_findings.title: EXPANSION stage unused and AT_RISK has no recovery path
cross_cutting_findings.description: LifecycleStage::EXPANSION is defined but never triggered. Users who enter AT_RISK (payment failure) have no automatic transition back to PAYING on recovery.
cross_cutting_findings.impact: Lifecycle funnel analytics show incomplete picture. AT_RISK users show as permanently at-risk even after payment recovery.
cross_cutting_findings.recommendation: 1) Add PAYING transition on invoice.payment_succeeded. 2) Add EXPANSION transition on plan upgrade.

cross_cutting_findings.title: Inconsistent dedup strategies across lifecycle commands
cross_cutting_findings.description: 5 commands use EmailSendLog (atomic unique constraint), 1 uses notification table query (win-back), 1 uses time-window (incomplete payments), 1 uses AuditLog existence (trials). No unified pattern.
cross_cutting_findings.impact: Win-back dedup loads all notifications in memory; time-window dedup is fragile on scheduler restart.
cross_cutting_findings.recommendation: Standardize on EmailSendLog pattern for all campaign-type emails

cross_cutting_findings.title: analytics-thresholds.php partially consumed
cross_cutting_findings.description: churn_rate and mrr_drop_percent thresholds defined in config but not checked by AdminHealthAlertCommand. Only trial_conversion threshold is actively used.
cross_cutting_findings.recommendation: Wire remaining thresholds into health alert system
